prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with an outdoor coastal/nature image. The first frame shows a textured, rocky or plant-covered surface, with the scene appearing grainy and greenish. An archival “MyFootage.com” watermark, an ID number, and a timecode overlay are visible.

It then cuts to waves crashing against a large dark rock or mound near the shore. Water sprays upward and across the rock, emphasizing surf and natural motion.

The next shots shift to close-ups of a piece of audio or television equipment. One frame shows a wooden or wood-grain cabinet surface with a long rectangular control panel and several round knobs or dials aligned along one side. Another close-up shows a vertical scale or tuning display beside three round knobs, suggesting radio/television controls. Part of a dark circular or oval object is visible at the right edge, but its full form is unclear.

The commercial then shows a home interior with a large console television or cabinet-style set placed in front of wide windows. Through the windows, ocean waves are visible outside. The room also contains chairs or side furniture, giving the setting a living-room or lounge-like appearance.

After this, the imagery returns to coastal scenery: a sloping shoreline with waves washing along the beach, followed by a rocky cliffside with surf breaking at its base. These shots continue the ocean/nature motif.

The final sampled frame displays a centered RCA logo inside a circular graphic. Beneath it is the visible text: “The Most Trusted Name in Sound.” The frame retains the same archival watermark, ID number, and timecode overlay.
